translation.en: Oh, the sky is so beautiful!

exampleSentencesTranslated: Oh, I forgot today is your birthday.
Oh, it’s raining so heavily, I’ll have to wait a while before going home.
Oh, this dish is so delicious, I’ve never tried it before.

example.vi: Ôi, trời ơi, thật tuyệt vời!

definitions.en: An expression of surprise or admiration.
definitions.vi: Một biểu hiện của sự ngạc nhiên hoặc ngưỡng mộ.
definitions.es: Una expresión de sorpresa o admiración.

notes:
Ôi means “oh!” — you use it to show sudden emotion like surprise, shock, happiness, sadness, or disappointment.

It’s an exclamation, usually at the beginning of a sentence to react to something.  

The feeling depends on tone and context — it can be positive (“wow!”) or negative (“oh no!”).  
Close cousin: ôi trời ơi (stronger, like “oh my God”).
exampleSentences: Ôi, em quên mất hôm nay là sinh nhật của anh.
Ôi, trời mưa to quá, chắc phải đợi một lúc mới về được.
Ôi, món ăn này ngon quá, chị chưa từng thử trước đây.
notes_md:
**Ôi** means “oh!” — you use it to show sudden emotion like surprise, shock, happiness, sadness, or disappointment.

It’s an exclamation, usually at the beginning of a sentence to react to something.  

The feeling depends on tone and context — it can be positive (“wow!”) or negative (“oh no!”).  
Close cousin: **ôi trời ơi** (stronger, like “oh my God”).
